campaign_goal: Validate the pg_enigma campaign harness on the fixed `frontier_rebase/pr1394/train_gpt_human.py` base by enforcing downstream-only control-paired comparison first, then testing same-trace checkpoint/export selection, while carrying recurrence handoff and shared-trunk late branching as deferred next mechanisms once the harness exposes the needed primitives.
name: Control-Paired Delta Admission On Downstream Sliding Score
why_keep: It fixes the comparison law for the whole cycle and directly addresses pack-specific noise on the real downstream lane.
minimal_probe: Run one 8-slot frontier selector pack with 2 exact controls, 2 replay-equivalent control clones, and 4 novel slots. Admit only by pack-local delta to paired controls on downstream `quantized_sliding_window` `score_bpb`.
expected_signal: Control clones define a stable noise band and at least one non-control clears that band on downstream `score_bpb` without relying on pre-quant ranking.
pack_rationale: Run first because every later executable solo pack should inherit this admission law instead of absolute-score ranking.

source_candidate.worldview: Keep the published pr1394 SP8192/GPTQ-embeddings/depth-recurrence/MuonEq-R/SDClip stack frozen and treat this cycle as a harness-validity search over bounded comparison, selector, and transition programs. Favor families that change the unit of comparison or artifact/transition policy on the real base, and mark stateful controller or branching lines as blocked when the current target script and harness do not yet expose the needed primitives.
source_candidate.name: Control-Paired Delta Admission On Downstream Sliding Score
source_candidate.mechanism: Replace raw ranking by control-paired delta ranking: each candidate is admitted and promoted only by its improvement over matched controls on downstream quantized sliding-window `score_bpb`, with pre-quant `val_bpb` used only as a guardrail rather than as a co-equal ranking metric.
source_candidate.broken_invariant: Absolute downstream means from a fresh pr1394 pack are stable enough to rank families without explicitly estimating pack-specific control spread.
source_candidate.not_local_tuning_reason: This changes the unit of comparison for the whole campaign and therefore which families survive at all; it does not retune any model scalar inside the pr1394 stack.
source_candidate.measurement_plan: Replay-equivalent controls establish a tight delta band, and real winners exceed that band on downstream `score_bpb` even when pre-quant improvements are smaller.
source_candidate.smallest_decisive_probe: Run one 8-slot admission pack with 2 exact controls, 2 replay-equivalent control clones, and 4 novel candidates. Promote only if a novel candidate clears the control-clone noise floor on downstream `score_bpb`.
source_candidate.expected_observable: Replay-equivalent controls establish a tight delta band, and real winners exceed that band on downstream `score_bpb` even when pre-quant improvements are smaller.
source_candidate.falsifier: If control clones rank as winners or their spread is comparable to the claimed gains from novel candidates, the pack is not learning enough to justify family promotion.
source_candidate.novelty_vs_history: This is distinct from prior patch-pack search because it changes campaign comparison semantics instead of introducing another model edit. It directly addresses the recorded failure mode of single-metric ranking under unstable pack conditions.
source_candidate.self_check_summary: Consequential because it changes campaign admission, not a local threshold. The probe is cheap and decisive, the lane is explicitly downstream, and the executability claim stays within control/candidate packing the harness already appears to support.

reasons: This is the strongest harness-reset line because it changes the unit of comparison, not the model.
Control-paired delta admission is the right answer to pack-specific noise and directly serves the downstream-only campaign law.
The probe is cheap, decisive, and executable without inventing new model-side machinery.
